ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sentiment_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment record */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the sentiment was recorded */,
  "polarity" REAL /* Polarity score indicating sentiment positivity or negativity */,
  "subjectivity" REAL /* Subjectivity score indicating personal opinion vs factual statements */,
  "bad" REAL /* Score indicating the presence of bad sentiment */,
  "depressed" REAL /* Score indicating the presence of depressed sentiment */,
  "encouraged" REAL /* Score indicating the presence of encouraged sentiment */,
  "happy" REAL /* Score indicating the presence of happy sentiment */,
  "joy" REAL /* Score indicating the presence of joy sentiment */,
  "loving" REAL /* Score indicating the presence of loving sentiment */,
  "content_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the content being analyzed */,
  "content_type" TEXT /* Type of content (e.g., post, comment, message) */,
  "source_platform" TEXT /* Source social media platform (e.g., Twitter, Facebook, Instagram) */,
  "user_handle" TEXT /* Handle or identifier of the user who generated the content */,
  "method_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sentiment analysis method used for this record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiment_records_method_id FOREIGN KEY ("method_id") REFERENCES sentiment_analysis_methods ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "emotions" (
  "emotion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each emotion */,
  "emotion_name" TEXT /* Name of the emotion category (e.g., happy, sad) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the emotion signifies */,
  "emotion_color" TEXT /* Color code to represent the emotion visually in reports and visualizations */,
  "emotion_icon" TEXT /* Icon or symbol to represent the emotion visually in reports and visualizations */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("emotion_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, admin) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user for contact purposes */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* User account status (e.g., active, suspended, deleted) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time of access */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the platform */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the platform */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Unique session identifier for the user's session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the report was created */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Content or data of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., trend analysis, sentiment summary) */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, published, archived) */,
  "report_title" TEXT /* Title of the report */,
  "report_summary" TEXT /* Summary or abstract of the report */,
  "method_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sentiment analysis method used for the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_reports_method_id FOREIGN KEY ("method_id") REFERENCES sentiment_analysis_methods ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trending_topics" (
  "topic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trending topic */,
  "topic_name" TEXT /* Name of the trending topic */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Overall sentiment score for the topic */,
  "recorded_time" TEXT /* Time when the topic was recorded as trending */,
  "topic_category" TEXT /* Category of the topic (e.g., politics, entertainment, health) */,
  "source_platform" TEXT /* Source social media platform where the topic is trending */,
  "user_count" INTEGER /* Number of users discussing the topic */,
  "content_count" INTEGER /* Number of pieces of content related to the topic */,
  "sentiment_trend" TEXT /* Trend of sentiment over time (e.g., increasing, decreasing, stable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("topic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiment_analysis_methods" (
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each method */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Name of the sentiment analysis method */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of how the method works */,
  "accuracy_score" REAL /* Accuracy score of the method */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the method */,
  "model_version" TEXT /* Version of the model used by the method */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the method (e.g., active, deprecated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user providing feedback */,
  "feedback_text" TEXT /* Content of the feedback */,
  "submitted_at" TEXT /* Date and time the feedback was submitted */,
  "feedback_type" TEXT /* Type of feedback (e.g., technical issue, feature request, general comment) */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the feedback (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolution_status" TEXT /* Status of the feedback resolution (e.g., pending, resolved, rejected) */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user assigned to resolve the feedback */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
